description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white surface, interspersed with subtle gray and brown veining and patches. The rock has a rough, natural texture with uneven facets and edges, suggesting it has not been artificially shaped or polished. Its surface appears weathered, with some areas showing more pronounced discoloration and darker mineral deposits. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one side slightly more sloped and another more angular, giving it a rugged appearance. The coloration and texture are consistent with rocks commonly found in landscaped urban parks or as natural barriers along sidewalks. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of human modification on its surface.
description: An irregular, white and gray rock with rough, weathered surfaces and natural mineral veining. Its asymmetrical shape and rugged texture make it suitable as a natural urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's angular side with prominent gray veining and a slightly sloped top surface.
description_view_1: The rock appears flatter and broader from this angle, with more white surface visible and subtle brownish patches.
description_view_2: This perspective shows a rugged, uneven face with deeper crevices and a mix of white and gray coloration.
description_view_3: The rock's back side is more rounded and smooth, with a dominant white surface and minimal visible veining.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
